metadata.title: TRINITY-2: The Governed Action — Multi-Root Quorum ExecutionProof over a Simulated High-Impact Authorization
metadata.description: TRINITY-2: a preregistered demonstration in which a simulated high-impact enterprise action (a $500,000 payment authorization) is allowed, held, or denied by a single Bell-CHSH nonclassicality witness independently evaluated on three distinct IBM Quantum processors (ibm_kingston, ibm_fez, ibm_marrakesh — all Heron r2) and cryptographically fused into one chain-linked, independently reconstructable ExecutionProof record. A preregistered quorum rule (≥2 of 3) with substitution/failover governs processor unavailability, and full calibration-snapshot hashing binds each device's submission-time state. Live-hardware result: D1 ibm_kingston S=2.7650, D2 ibm_fez S=2.7235, D3 ibm_marrakesh S=2.7235 (all PASS); quorum 3/3, not degraded; aggregate ExecutionProof verdict ALLOW → governed action AUTHORIZED; trinity_certified=True; independent verifier PASS. The payment is SIMULATED — no real funds, rail, or authority. Chain-linked to TRINITY-1 (record_hash 8d23accac81791fa10f1dba1be79a132168966c4620fc42d16de656bcf9d688b). Scope & honesty: this is experimental evidence, device-dependent, common-provider (three independent IBM devices, not independent vendors); it is NOT device-independent, NOT loophole closure, and NOT inter-processor entanglement. Coherent Inheritance Framework (CIF) · Remnant Fieldworks Inc.
